title: Author canonical pheno-fastapi-base Python package (FastAPI app factory + structlog access log + AppError handler + AsyncTestClient)
author: forge

downstream: L2-23 (PhenoCompose polyglot stack — any FastAPI service built on the pheno-* fleet)

summary: Authored the canonical pheno-fastapi-base Python package: a single, opinionated FastAPI base for the pheno-* HTTP service fleet. Public surface is 6 symbols: create_app(title, version) -> FastAPI (app factory wiring /healthz + /readyz probes, StructlogAccessLogMiddleware, AppError handler, and a lifespan that emits structlog.info('app.started', title=..., version=...)); AppError(code, message, *, details=None, status=None) with a stable string code; code_to_status = {NotFound: 404, Validation: 422, Conflict: 409, Storage: 500, Domain: 500} with DEFAULT_STATUS=500 for unknown codes; StructlogAccessLogMiddleware (BaseHTTPMiddleware emitting one JSON access line per request); and AsyncTestClient (httpx.AsyncClient subclass pre-wired with httpx.ASGITransport, constructed via with_app(app) classmethod).

public_api.create_app: pheno_fastapi_base.app.create_app(title: str, version: str) -> FastAPI — app factory. Calls configure_structlog(title, version), instantiates FastAPI(title=title, version=version, lifespan=_lifespan), stashes title/version on app.state, installs StructlogAccessLogMiddleware, registers the AppError handler, and registers GET /healthz (200, {"status": "ok"}) + GET /readyz (200, {"status": "ready"}). The lifespan context manager emits structlog.info('app.started', title=app.title, version=app.version) on startup and a matching app.stopped on shutdown.
public_api.configure_structlog: pheno_fastapi_base.app.configure_structlog(title: str, version: str) -> None — Idempotent structlog setup. Configures the global processor chain (merge_contextvars, add_log_level, TimeStamper iso-utc, StackInfoRenderer, format_exc_info, JSONRenderer) with cache_logger_on_first_use=False (so tests can reconfigure the chain), and binds service='pheno-fastapi-base' + title + version into structlog contextvars. create_app calls this internally.
public_api.AppError: pheno_fastapi_base.errors.AppError(code: str, message: str, *, details: dict | None = None, status: int | None = None) — Exception subclass carrying a stable string code, a human message, optional structured details, and an optional explicit status override. str(exc) renders as 'CODE: message' for log grep-ability. __repr__ returns 'AppError(code=..., message=...)'.
public_api.code_to_status: pheno_fastapi_base.errors.code_to_status: dict[str, int] (Final) — the canonical 'code -> HTTP status' map. Keys: NotFound=404, Validation=422, Conflict=409, Storage=500, Domain=500. Unknown codes fall back to DEFAULT_STATUS=500.
public_api.DEFAULT_STATUS: pheno_fastapi_base.errors.DEFAULT_STATUS: int (Final) = 500 — returned by app_error_handler when exc.code is not in code_to_status and exc.status is not set.
public_api.app_error_handler: pheno_fastapi_base.errors.app_error_handler(request, exc) -> JSONResponse — registered for AppError. Status resolution order: exc.status (if set) > code_to_status[exc.code] (if code is known) > DEFAULT_STATUS (500). Response body is {"error": {"code": ..., "message": ..., "details": ...}} with details omitted when empty. If invoked with a non-AppError (defensive), returns a generic 500.
public_api.register_error_handlers: pheno_fastapi_base.errors.register_error_handlers(app: FastAPI) -> None — convenience wrapper that calls app.add_exception_handler(AppError, app_error_handler).
public_api.StructlogAccessLogMiddleware: pheno_fastapi_base.middleware.StructlogAccessLogMiddleware — starlette.middleware.base.BaseHTTPMiddleware subclass. dispatch() binds method + path into structlog contextvars, calls the next handler, then emits structlog.info('app.access', status_code, duration_ms, client?) at INFO (or .exception(...) on uncaught error, then re-raises). duration_ms is a float rounded to 3 decimals. The middleware is in the public surface so consumers can subclass it for request_id correlation.
public_api.AsyncTestClient: pheno_fastapi_base.testing.AsyncTestClient — httpx.AsyncClient subclass. __init__ accepts (app, *, base_url='http://testserver', headers=None, cookies=None, timeout=None, **kwargs) and pre-wires httpx.ASGITransport(app=app, raise_app_exceptions=True) so the client drives the app in-process. with_app(app, **kwargs) is the classmethod preferred construction. Convenience: new_request_id() and an async request_id() context manager that set/clear an X-Test-Request-Id header.

dependencies.httpx: >=0.27 (resolved: 0.28.1) — required because AsyncTestClient is part of the public surface; the spec implicitly requires a test-client, and AsyncTestClient IS the test-client.
dependencies.pytest-asyncio (dev): >=0.23 (resolved: 1.4.0); configured in pyproject.toml with asyncio_mode='auto' so async tests run without per-test decorators

test_results.command: cd pheno-fastapi-base && python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate && pip install -e ".[dev]" && pytest -q (verbatim from the task brief; re-run by this agent)

test_coverage: test_app_started_event_emitted (tests/test_app.py:64) — drives app.router.lifespan_context directly (httpx.ASGITransport does NOT propagate ASGI lifespan events; documented in the testing.py class docstring), captures structlog events via the captured_events fixture with .reapply() called after create_app() clobbers the processor chain, and asserts at least one event has event='app.started', title=<expected>, version=<expected>

test_app_error_handler_returns_404_for_not_found (tests/test_app.py:107) — registers a GET route that raises AppError('NotFound', '...', details={'entity': 'User', 'id': '42'}), asserts 404 + body exactly {'error': {'code': 'NotFound', 'message': '...', 'details': {...}}} and that code_to_status['NotFound'] == 404

test_app_error_handler_returns_422_for_validation (tests/test_app.py:146) — registers a POST route that raises AppError('Validation', '...', details={'field': 'email', 'value': 'not-an-email'}), asserts 422 + body['error'] keys (code, message, details) match and code_to_status['Validation'] == 422

test_code_to_status_contains_required_keys (tests/test_app.py:181) — non-async sanity that asserts code_to_status == {NotFound: 404, Validation: 422, Conflict: 409, Storage: 500, Domain: 500} exactly, so an accidental deletion of a required key is caught

design_decisions.flat_layout_no_src: Package is a flat-layout project at pheno-fastapi-base/ (no src/ parent). Build backend: hatchling. This matches the L2-27 pheno-cargo-template convention for 'canonical shared library' packages — the directory IS the package, no nested layer of indirection. (Rust sibling crates are forced flat by an empty [workspace] table in their own Cargo.toml; for Python the same effect comes from the [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages list.)
design_decisions.AsyncTestClient_is_public: The brief lists AsyncTestClient as part of the contract, so httpx is a runtime dep (not just a dev dep). This is a deliberate API choice: every downstream pheno-* FastAPI service needs the same test client, and lifting it into the base package means a one-import upgrade path for the fleet.
design_decisions.explicit_status_override: AppError accepts an optional status= argument that wins over code_to_status. This lets a service surface a one-off 503 from a 'Domain' code (or any other tweak) without modifying the global map. The default path (status=None) still flows through the canonical map, so the contract holds for 99% of cases.
design_decisions.details_omitted_when_empty: The handler omits the 'details' key from the response body when exc.details is empty. Clients can rely on the shape {"error": {"code": ..., "message": ...}} for the common case and {"error": {"code": ..., "message": ..., "details": {...}}} for the structured case. The tests pin both shapes exactly.
design_decisions.raise_app_exceptions_True: AsyncTestClient passes raise_app_exceptions=True to httpx.ASGITransport so handler exceptions propagate out of `await client.get(...)` (matching Starlette's sync TestClient behavior). Tests that want to assert on a 500-style error from a handler use pytest.raises(...). The alternative (swallowing handler exceptions) would silently break assertion logic in tests that expect a 500.
design_decisions.configure_structlog_called_in_create_app: create_app() calls configure_structlog() internally so callers don't have to remember to do it. The downside is that create_app() clobbers any prior structlog processor chain — the test fixture exposes a .reapply() method to recover. The upside is that a service using create_app() gets the canonical JSON formatter with zero ceremony.

gotchas: httpx.ASGITransport does NOT propagate ASGI lifespan events. The test_app_started_event_emitted test therefore drives app.router.lifespan_context directly via 'async with app.router.lifespan_context(app):' instead. This matches the FastAPI/Starlette doc pattern for testing lifespans out-of-band. Documented in the testing.py class docstring and the test docstring.

structlog's cache_logger_on_first_use=True (the default) captures a BoundLogger on the first get_logger() call and subsequent structlog.configure() calls are no-ops for that logger. configure_structlog() therefore sets cache_logger_on_first_use=False. The cost is a tiny per-call BoundLogger construction (single-digit microseconds per structlog measurements); the benefit is that the test suite can swap the processor chain between cases via the captured_events fixture.

create_app() calls configure_structlog(), which overwrites the structlog global processor chain. The captured_events fixture therefore exposes a .reapply() method that re-installs the capture processor chain. Tests that drive create_app() and then capture events must call captured_events.reapply() between create_app() and the lifespan entry. The /healthz, /readyz, and AppError tests don't need to capture events so they don't touch the fixture.
consolidation_targets: FastAPI service templates in the pheno-* fleet — any service that currently hand-rolls its own structlog setup, error handler, and httpx test client can drop all of that in favor of `from pheno_fastapi_base import create_app` + `app = create_app(title, version)`.
downstream_consumers: L2-23 (PhenoCompose polyglot stack) — any Python HTTP service in the stack imports from pheno_fastapi_base
L5 (pheno-* HTTP service crates) — same, when the L5 service is in Python

notes: L3-#51 is the first Python L3 crate in the fleet — every other L3 sibling (L3-#46, L3-#47, L3-#48, L3-#49, L3-#50, L3-#52-#57) is either Rust or Go. The L3-#51 package follows the same design rules: standalone package, no fleet-registry entry, branch off main, local-only, one tree per task. The Python-specific decisions are: (1) flat layout (no src/) matching the L2-27 pheno-cargo-template convention; (2) hatchling build backend (the same backend the pheno-worklog-schema sibling uses); (3) PEP 561 typed (ships py.typed); (4) AsyncTestClient as a runtime dep (not a dev dep) because the test client is part of the public surface; (5) pytest-asyncio in asyncio_mode='auto' so the test file doesn't need per-test decorators. The verification was re-run by this agent end-to-end (cd pheno-fastapi-base && python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate && pip install -e '.[dev]' && pytest -q) and matches the prior agent's result: 6/6 tests pass, ruff clean, package imports cleanly with all 6 public symbols.
